text:
Admission Date:  2011-09-20              [**Year/Month/Day **] Date:   1997-08-02

Date of Birth:  1989-08-04             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Trazodone

Attending:Alexander
Chief Complaint:
Altered mental status in the setting of Suicidal attempt via
overdose

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

History of Present Illness:
47 year old Female with history of depression/anxiety who
presented to the MICU with altered mental status and difficulty
waking up in the setting of possible overdose and suicide
attempt.
According to report from the husband patient was doing well on
ECT. On night prior to admission, went to an improv comedy show
of her daughter. Belinda Castro noticed her affect was strange in getting
home.

Morning of admission, patient was difficult to rouse from bed.
He states patient was breathing, but very lethargic. She was
thus rushed to the ED. She denied overdoses(which is consistent
with prior overdoses when the pt only admits after tox screen is
positive). Husband did find a pill that she is not supposed to
have, and commented it looked like some of the Patricia Stanley pills she's
obtained from the street. (per husband pt is no longer
prescribed Patricia Stanley but buys it over the internet. Has even opened
up a new credit card and PO box to secretly order the
medication)

When patient spoke to psychiatry she did admit to taking Patricia Stanley
and Lorazepam with clear suicidal attempt. She continues to to
endorse SI. Denies hallucination, current confusion. Denies EtOH
or other drugs in recent weeks. She reports having received ECT
at Trinity Medical Center - 7th Street Campus - Dba Trinity Moline while admitted in 19.

Past Medical History:
- Depression/Anxiety with multiple prior suicide attempts
(recent hospitalization at Centura Health-porter Adventist Hospital Psychiatry Unit in
11/2010)
- Chronic ETOH Dependence; h/o alcoholism years ago, per husband

- Prescription drug abuse (abuse of Ativan and [**Year (4 digits) 3461**] in the past)
- MAO-I toxicity 1979-10-01 during which time she developed ATN,
acute liver injury, and mildly depressed LVEF 55% (just had
repeat stress test at Cincinnati Children's Hospital Medical Center At Lindner Center this week).
- Hypothyrodisim in past but stopped taking levothyroxine

Social History:
- Tobacco: No current tobacco use.
- EtOH: Prior alcoholism history, husband reports she has not
had problems with alcohol abuse in years.
- Illicit Drugs: Per husband, denies known history of illicit
drug abuse. However has h/o prescription medication abuse,
including benzodiazepines and San Joaquin Community Hospital. Prior OB/GYN physician at
Cincinnati Children's Hospital Medical Center At Lindner Center, trained at HMS for med school and Copper Springs Hospital Inc for residency, now
moving to a different career, has not practiced for 1 year.
Married with 2 kids, lives with husband at home.


Family History:
Patient is adopted

Brief Hospital Course:
47 yo F with h/o depression on ECT with prior overdoses and
suicide attempts presenting with AMS concerning and suspicious
for attempted suicide via overdose

# Metabolic Encephalopathy, Suicidal Ingestion, Suicidal
Ideation:
Most likely suicidal attempt in the setting of overdose. Earlier
head CT for Encephalopathy ruled out any intracranial
abnormality. Initially patient denied overdose, however, after
psychiatric interview she stated that she had intentionaly
overdosed on [**Month/Day/Year 3461**].  Labs and blood tox on presentation were
normal but patient admits to taking [**Month/Day/Year 3461**] and possibly lorazepam.
 After evaluation from the psych team, a psych admission was
deemed necessary. She had a 1:1 sitter while on the floor for
suicidality. Psych meds were held until better clarification
with her pharmacist. She was placed on lorazepam for anxiety and
ambien to help with sleep.  Medications were reconciled,
cymbalta restarted at 60 mg daily due to concern of possible
withdrawal with psychiatry consultation.  She required ativan as
needed for anxiety.  She is medically stable and cleared for
[**Month/Day/Year **] pending inpatient psychiatry placement.

# Hypothyroidism:
With moderately low T4 and normal low T3. Concern for central
process but patient also claims to be taking synthroid which
could explain these results. She does have a history of
hypothyroidism and has stopped taking synthroid recently.
Initially, this was held in house while attempting to confirm
home meds and restarted prior to [**Month/Day/Year **].

Full Code

Patient transferred to inpatient psychiatry unit


Medications on Admission:
1. Cymbalta 60mg daily
2. Clonazepam
3. Dextramphetamine
4. Topamax
5. Abilify 5 mg San Joaquin Community Hospital
6. Levothyroxine 25 mcg daily
7. Zolpidem 12.5mg PO QHS
8. Oral contraceptive
9. Stool softener


San Joaquin Community Hospital Medications:
1. Duloxetine 30 mg PO TID
2. Levothyroxine Sodium 25 mcg PO DAILY
3. Ambien CR *NF* (zolpidem) 12.5 mg Oral QHS


San Joaquin Community Hospital Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Silver Hill Hospital, Inc. Hospital - 106 Nicole Turnpike
New Christina, ID 47813

USS Ramos
FPO AA 07854-4087 Instructions:
Dear Mrs Mcdowell,

You were admitted because you presented with altered mental
status in the setting of a suicide attempt via overdose on Cox.
You are currently stable and being transferred to psychiatry.
All your medications were held while inpatient pending
verification of doses.

You are currently on only
Zolpidem CR 12.5mg PO QHS
Lorazepam- 0.5mg PO BID PRN
Cymbalta - 60mg PO daily

Temporary Medication changes:
Cymbalta 90mg changed to 60mg

Medications temporarily stopped while inpatient:
Prazosin 2mg 1 QHS
Abilify 5mg once a day
Topiramate 100mg - twice a day
Clonazepma 1mg 1 daily PRN
Metoclopramide -10mg 1 tab before ECT
Famotidine - 1 tab before ECT
Levothyroxine - 25mg once a day
